sourceMovie.title: Thank You for Smoking
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Thank You for Smoking works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Thank You for Smoking clicked for you, The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Thank You for Smoking?
faq.answer: Try The Wolf of Wall Street, The Royal Tenenbaums, Coffee and Cigarettes—each shares DNA with Thank You for Smoking while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Thank You for Smoking?
faq.answer: Thank You for Smoking (2005) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Thank You for Smoking worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
